name: ING
mission: The entire experience of planning a series of seminars with ING was inspirational. They did an amazing job. The ING team welcomed input along the way which allowed each session to be even better than the one before it. We will continue to work with ING as I am confident that they will continue to provide value in our work.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The mission centers on religious and cultural education, which may have lower urgency for donors focused on humanitarian crises [5]. While it offers sadaqah-eligible giving opportunities, the alignment score of 24/50 reflects a moderate fit for general donors interested in interfaith harmony rather than direct social services [7][8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through educational presentations and website resources [3][6]. However, with a program expense ratio of 39.5% and a low impact score of 14/50, there is limited evidence of high-efficiency direct impact or third-party verification of its long-term effectiveness [1][2].

strengths.detail: The organization operates with a <cite id="9">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 spent per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that donor acquisition costs do not deplete contributions.
ING maintains <cite id="10">5.2 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient buffer for its educational programs.

strengths_deep_dive: Islamic Networks Group has built a robust infrastructure for interfaith dialogue over three decades. Their impact is evidenced by a <cite id="14">96% satisfaction rate among educators</cite> and an <cite id="15">89% effectiveness rating</cite> for their speakers. Beyond live events, which reached <cite id="16">400,000 attendees in 2020</cite>, the organization has successfully transitioned to digital delivery, garnering <cite id="17">870,000 views of recorded talks</cite>. This multi-channel approach allows them to maintain a <cite id="18">projected annual reach of 2 million</cite> individuals across various sectors of society.
rich_narrative.summary: Islamic Networks Inc, also known as Islamic Networks Group (ING), is a long-standing educational non-profit that has been <cite id="1">operating since 1993</cite> to foster peace and cross-cultural understanding. The organization specializes in <cite id="2">educational outreach and interfaith engagement</cite>, primarily through its Islamic Speakers Bureau and specialized training modules. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $622,869</cite>, reflecting a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 6.0%</cite>. While ING maintains a significant digital footprint, with <cite id="5">50,000 monthly website visitors</cite> and a projected <cite id="6">annual reach of 2 million people</cite>, its financial structure is characterized by a <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 39.5%</cite>. This ratio is notably lower than the <cite id="8">peer program ratio median of 85.3%</cite> for similar religious and cultural organizations. Despite this, the charity demonstrates high fundraising efficiency, reporting <cite id="9">$0.00 spent per $1 raised</cite>, and maintains a stable financial cushion with <cite id="10">5.2 months of working capital</cite>. Their work includes hosting <cite id="11">780 interfaith panels</cite> and training <cite id="12">200 Muslim teens</cite> in a single year to navigate cultural challenges and promote inclusion.
